title: The Macro Minute:  Whose stock market and economy is it—Biden’s or Trump’s?

Happy Thursday out there at Te42. It's
your skipper here, Darius Dell to
present our macro minute for Thursday,
May 1st, 2025. As always, we'll start
with the executive summary from today's
leadoff morning note. If you would like
to review the analysis supporting these
conclusions and to see if you need to
make any changes in your portfolio to
remain on the right side of market risk,
you need to be a client of 42 macro. So
today's key macro question is whose
stock market and economy is it, Biden's
or Trump's? Our answers are US trade
representative uh Jameson Greer told Fox
News that the US is close to announcing
a first trunch of trade deals that
includes tariff reductions on certain
partners. Substantial further progress
on the trade negotiation front aligns
with our consistently sanguin take
regarding the trade/c capital war since
April 9th. If the Trump administration
can acknowledge the fact that while the
art of the deal may work in trade
negotiations, the damage strategic
ambiguity is doing to the economy and
uncertainty up equals GDP down terms is
not worth the risk of a full-blown
recession. They'd be much better served
to state their intentions plainly. This
would allow markets to look through any
tariff-driven economic downturn with an
eye towards a high probability recovery
in 2026.
As far as markets are concerned, tariffs
aren't the problem. It's the uncertainty
and constant flip-flopping regarding
tariffs that is holding the stock market
back from aggressively pricing in the
golden age that was promised on the
campaign trail. With the right messaging
from the administration, a V-shaped
market in a U-shaped economy can easily
become the modal outcome in this
historically wide distribution of
probable economic policy and market
outcomes. President Trump and Secretary
Scott Bessant, if you're still reading
one of you, uh, please just tell us
exactly how much pain to expect so that
Wall Street can properly kitchen sink
its forecast and begin pricing in the
2026 recovery. Respectfully, your poor
communication is what's holding the
economy and asset markets back from the
boom that you guys promised.
Transitioning to our 42 micro dashboard,
as always, we'll wrap up with a question
from our community. This one's titled
modal outcome/base case. Says, "Hello,
Skipper. Is the Wshaped market or
revisit of the lows seen in early April
no longer the modal outcome or base case
to 42 macro? When do we buy with both
hands?" So, uh, uh, good question. I'll
answer it in reverse. Uh, you buy when
both hands when Kiss and Dr. Mo tell you
to buy with both hands. You do not buy
ever when Darius tells you to buy
because Darius is not going to tell you
to buy. Again, I say this every day and
I'm going to repeat. We do not use
fundamental research here at 42 macro to
tell our clients what they should be
doing in their portfolios. Now is 100%
of our clients aren't following Kiss and
Dr. Mo. Obviously, we have, you know, a
collective accumulated 25 plus trillion
dollars of institutional client AUM that
we advise on with our research. So
clearly they're not all here, you know,
trading$2 trillion dollars plus worth of
portfolios with KISS and Dr. Mo. not
that's not ever going to be the case.
How so, you know, we respect the fact
that our institutional investor clients
are going to do what they do and that
we're just merely an input to their
investment process and we're grateful to
be that input as it relates to the
retail investors whom the thousands of
you that we're helping retire on time
and comfortably. You need to be focused
on KISS and Dr. Mo as it relates to what
you should be doing in your portfolio.
When I talk fundamental research like
W-shaped market in a U-shaped economy or
V-shaped market in a U-shaped economy,
that's merely with the expectation to
get you to understand the full
distribution of probable economic market
and policy outcomes so that when KISS
and Dr. Mo tell you to do something
different in your portfolio, KISS a
couple times a month, Dr. Mo, pick your
factor, you know, then you feel
comfortable making that change in your

know, it's a lot easier to
dispassionately execute a completely
systematic rack of risk management
overlay like KISS or Dr. Mo if you have
some expectation of what's likely to
occur fundamentally. And moreover, it's

don't know. I genuinely don't know if

probable outcomes. Now, I'm not so sure.
And it's and guess what? It's okay. You
don't have to have answers every day as
an investor. It's a ridicul that that's
a a a preposterous
expectation that is perpetuated by an

relatively evenly distributed and that's

future like I actually know right now. I
tell you that the the tales are are
relatively evenly distributed. So there

is okay he is incrementally

think is some of the best fundamental
research in the world. And again, I
won't make any I won't, you know, I
won't mince words about that. It is
true. Just look at our track record. Um,
but again, our our clients here at 42
Macro do not use that or shouldn't be
using that in my opinion. Uh, they can

with their own money. But in my opinion,
the best thing to do as it relates to
using 42 macro research and risk
management signals to create great
outcomes in your portfolio is to just
dispassionately execute the kiss and
doctor most signals when the kiss and
doctor mode signals tell you to do
something different in your portfolio.
And again, that fundamental research
overlay to those signals is just merely
me trying to, you know, do what I used
to do as an offensive lineman, which is
pave the way, you know, for you mentally
and emotionally so that you feel
comfortable and confident and
dispassionate enough to make the signal
changes without any sort of um without
any stress stress involved. You know,
our fundamental research is designed to
de-stress you so that in the future when
KISS and Dr. Mo tell you to do something
different, you're ready to do something
different emotionally. And that's all I
think it's a fantastic process. You
know, this is what the world's best, you
know, pot shop hedge funds are doing um
that are amassing all the assets in the
institutional asset management business.
Um you know, this is what they do. Um
so, uh let you in on a little secret.
You if you're going to want to if you're
going to invest and you want to invest
well, you want to invest like the best.
You don't want to invest like a regular
person who's constantly being whipped
around by fundamental views and has no
real ability to risk manage, you know,
their fundamental views being wrong
without their portfolio declining in
value a significant amount. That's what
we're avoiding here by, you know,
respecting our kiss and Dr. Mo signals
uh and taking advantage of the the big
bull runs and the big longer term trends
in markets. That's that's our our entire
process is engineered to ensure that our
clients maximize upside capture in the
big bull runs. Our process is not at all
optimized for making sure that our
clients are on the right side of every
short-term trade in markets. That is not
what we're trying to do. If that's what
you're seeking, we will not be a good
research partner for you. And I would
imagine our very low low singledigit
attrition rate is from people who come
into our payw wall beyond our payw wall
behind our payw wall realize that

leave. That low singledigit attrition
rate is people walking in realizing that
oh this is not what I thought it was and
they leave. But the high 90s something
percent you know customer retention rate
that we have is a function of folks who
actually want to you know
dispassionately participate in bull
markets you know for with respect to the
medium and long term and capturing the
bulk of those uptrends and avoiding draw
down risk uh in bare markets and I think
